text: Carbohydrate and hydration recommendations for endurance athletes have remained relatively stable and consistent over recent years.
Protein and fat intake have been traditionally underemphasized in endurance athlete nutrition guidance despite their importance.
Nitrates, antioxidants, caffeine, and probiotics are among the few supplements with evidence of benefit for endurance performance.
Training in a low carbohydrate state ('train low') is a strategy that may enhance endurance adaptations, though recommendations remain nuanced.
The post-exercise nutritional recovery window remains an important period for optimizing adaptation and recovery in endurance athletes.
Most commercially available supplements lack sufficient evidence to support their use for endurance performance enhancement.
